item.title: FACES
item.description: My earliest complete body of pieces circa. 2016-2018 Over the course of two+ years, this collection of portraits were all drawn on various types of paper and with H, B2, B6, B7, and B8 drawing pencils, and with black & gray color pencils. During this period, I became interested in the human face and its portions. Although it is true that no two faces have exactly the same features, many faces do share a similar portion and and size.

item.title: Emphasis
item.description: Digitializing My Past Works When I moved back home for my last semester of university, I was sentimental and felt a little cheated that my senior year will have to be spent at home. While cleaning my stuff, I found a box of past works from high school. I picked three to redraw digitially and hopefully add a layer of depth that 16 year old me did not have at the time when I conceieved these drawings.

item.title: February 2021 Digital Pieces
item.description: Some Sketches in February All pieces were drawn in Adobe Fresco on an Ipad Pro.

item.title: March 2021 Digital Pieces
item.description: Some Sketches from March These were drawn before I need to take a break from drawing to focus on my senior capstone and courses.

item.title: Project 6 : Tableau Dashboard of My Spotify Data
item.description: Project Overview This project started from a desire to uncover more pattens in my music streaming data that Spotify does not present in their annual Wrapped Up. Essentially I wanted to explore how my streaming times and the music that I listened to changed with events in my life and the pandemic. From pure observation of using Spotify between my laptop and phone, I did noticed that I mostly listened to songs that were released two-three years back during this past year.

item.title: Project 5 : Airline Reservation Flask Web App
item.description: This project consisted of building a flask web app for an airline reservation system. The app is able to handle different signup and login for customers, booking agents, and airline staffs. The app also has different use cases for these three users. Customer Use Cases signup/login search for flights using the airport code and city name purchase flights view personal spending view past and upcoming flights Booking Agent Use Cases signup/login search for flights purchase flights for a customer view their commission for a specific range of time view their top customers by commission earned and tickets sold Airline Staff Use Cases signup/login searh for & view past and upcoming flights for their airline view top customers and agents view distribution of sales (direct vs indirect) add new planes, airports, & flights update flight status view top destintations Anonymous User Use Cases search for flights Project Details:

item.title: **Depression vs. Covid Fatigue**: Data Science Capstone
item.description: Motivation Since the onset of the pandemic, depression has risen, but so has covid fatigue and blues (general feeling of tiredness and sadness). Although the line between blues and depression can be blurry at times, the most obvious difference is in the length of their symptoms. Depressed feelings last much longer with occasional moments of joy, whereas blues can come and go in shorter episodes. Nonetheless, people affected by either pandemic blues or depression have found substantial support groups (online communities around a hashtag) on Twitter to voice their experiences and reassure others going through similar symptoms.

item.title: Project 4 : Korean Skincare Recommender
item.description: Data used in this project was scraped from the Soko Glam website in September 2020. The nice thing about this website that made it an ideal candidate for this project is that users can browse product listings based on skin type, thereby also making it easy for me to scrape data based on those collections. This is so that I don’t need to conduct additional research to determine if a product is best for which skin type (oily, dry, normal, combination.

item.title: Project 3 : Visualizing Tech Salaries
item.description: In this project, I extracted tech salary data from levels.fyi.com and created multiple data visualizations to compare salaries, bonuses, benefits, etc. across the FAANG companies. Project Details: Data Source Repo Link

item.title: Project 2: Exo Google Trends
item.description: Google Trends allows user to search for at most 5 keywords at a time. This project demostrates how to search for more than 5 terms at once using the Google Trends API in python. Project Details: I also wrote a medium article for this project. Repo Link

item.title: Project 1 : Airline Sentiment Classification
item.description: This project attempts to classify twitter tweets about US airline using Logistic Regression as negative, positive, or neutral. Project Details: The dataset is publicly available at Kaggle.com. Repo Link

item.title: Theme Features
item.description: Here are some helpful tips for setting up this theme. Configuration Most of what you’ll want to configure is demonstrated in the exampleSite config.toml. This is Hugo’s configuration file. You can copy the config.toml in the exampleSite/ to your site root get started. Here are all the options included in the configuration file for this example site! hello hello Syntax Highlighting Introduction allows the use of Hugo’s rich built-in syntax highlighting capabilities.
